title: JORS: prepare submission for lab_archives_mcp
JORS: address reviewer revisions and prepare resubmission
description: Umbrella epic for the Journal of Open Research Software revision round. Tracks manuscript corrections, response letter preparation, version/citation consistency, and final resubmission packaging required to answer the April 2026 reviewer and editor comments.
notes:
Recommended execution order:
1. Run lab_archives_mcp-2 and lab_archives_mcp-4 first in parallel. These are the evidence and metadata prerequisites that unblock later writing.
2. Revise the manuscript core in this order: lab_archives_mcp-5.2, lab_archives_mcp-5.3, lab_archives_mcp-5.1, then lab_archives_mcp-5.4 once the QC evidence from lab_archives_mcp-2 is available.
3. Treat lab_archives_mcp-5.5 and lab_archives_mcp-1.1.3 as optional polish after the compulsory reviewer items are covered.
4. Draft lab_archives_mcp-1.1.1 only after the required manuscript and metadata tasks are complete.
5. Leave lab_archives_mcp-1.1.2 as the final packaging and pre-upload step.
Estimated critical-path effort: roughly 10 to 12 hours excluding optional polish and upload time.

title: JORS: draft point-by-point response and cover letter
description: Draft the response-to-reviewers document and editor cover letter for the JORS resubmission. Address every reviewer point explicitly, identify where manuscript text changed, and justify any comment that is not addressed directly in the code or paper.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Every reviewer and editor comment is addressed individually in the response document.
2. Each response states either what changed and where, or why no change was made.
3. The cover letter summarizes the main revisions without contradicting the detailed point-by-point response.
4. The response package is specific enough that an editor can map comments to manuscript or repository changes quickly.
notes:
Recommended order: 6. Estimate: 120 min. Start only after the required manuscript and metadata tasks are finished.
[orchestrator] Upstream lab_archives_mcp-4 closed in RUN_ID=20260415-155153-db1d7e41 on run/20260415-155153-db1d7e41.

title: JORS: assemble revised upload package and submission checklist
description: Prepare the final resubmission package for upload to the JORS portal. Confirm the revised manuscript, figure assets, response-to-reviewers file, and any metadata or citation corrections are present and internally consistent before submission.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The revised manuscript, figures, and response-to-reviewers files are all present and named appropriately for JORS upload.
2. Version, citation, and DOI references are internally consistent across the uploaded materials.
3. The package includes the response document required by the editor and any revised figure assets referenced by the manuscript.
4. A final pre-upload check confirms there are no known missing compulsory reviewer items.
notes:
Recommended order: 7. Estimate: 45 min. Final step immediately before upload.

title: JORS: surface coverage reporting more clearly in README and CI summary
description: Optional improvement: make existing coverage collection and reporting more obvious in repository-facing documentation or badges so quality claims are easier for reviewers to verify.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Repository-facing documentation makes it obvious that coverage artifacts are already collected or explains clearly if they are not exposed publicly.
2. Any badge, README text, or CI-summary change introduced for this purpose remains accurate and non-inflated.
3. The optional improvement is kept separate from acceptance-critical JORS revisions if it cannot be completed in time.
notes: Recommended order: optional. Estimate: 45 min. Do only if the acceptance-critical tasks are already covered.

title: Implement Graph-Based Knowledge Navigation Tools
description: Add tools like get_related_pages and trace_provenance to the MCP server. These should leverage the ExperimentContext and Finding models to allow the agent to traverse the semantic graph of the research.
design: Use NetworkX graph in ProjectContext for traversal. 'get_related_pages' will query graph neighbors. 'trace_provenance' will query graph predecessors.

comments.author: samuelbrudner
comments.text: NOTE: Terminology update. 'ExperimentContext' has been renamed to 'ProjectContext' to support multi-notebook workflows.

title: Implement 'Ready-to-Work' Heuristics
description: Create a suggest_next_steps tool that analyzes the current SessionState (active experiment, recent findings) to propose logical next actions for the agent.

comments.author: samuelbrudner
comments.text: NOTE: Terminology update. 'Experiment' references should be read as 'Project' (e.g., active project context).

title: Refine Semantic Model Definitions
description: Review and expand the ExperimentContext and Finding Pydantic models in state.py. Ensure they capture sufficient metadata (e.g., confidence scores, evidence links) to support the graph traversal and heuristic tools.

title: Integrate NetworkX for Graph Modeling
description: Add 'networkx' as a project dependency to support formal graph modeling of the ProjectContext (nodes=pages, edges=links). This will power the graph navigation tools.

title: JORS: collect test and coverage evidence for revision
description: Collect and document the evidence needed to support revised quality-control claims in the JORS response. Include current test scope, CI behavior, integration-test strategy, any available coverage outputs, and what can be stated accurately in the manuscript and response letter.
acceptance_criteria:
1. A concrete inventory of current tests is documented, including the major unit, integration, and workflow areas covered.
2. The issue notes which checks run in GitHub Actions, on which operating systems and Python versions, and whether coverage artifacts are already produced.
3. Any statements proposed for the manuscript or response letter are limited to evidence that can be verified from the repository or CI configuration.
4. Remaining gaps are stated explicitly, including any metrics or manual-validation claims that still need to be gathered before submission.
notes:
Recommended order: 1. Estimate: 90 min. Can be done in parallel with lab_archives_mcp-4.

description: Resolve version and citation inconsistencies across the README citation block, manuscript metadata, release references, CITATION.cff, and archived Zenodo version references so the resubmission package presents a consistent version story.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The README citation block, manuscript metadata, release references, and archived-version language no longer contradict each other.
2. The accepted version story for the revision is written down clearly enough to reuse in the paper and response letter.
3. Any remaining difference between the current development version and the archived submission version is explained explicitly rather than implied.
4. The resubmission package can cite one coherent version/DOI trail without ambiguity.
notes:
Recommended order: 1. Estimate: 60 min. Can be done in parallel with lab_archives_mcp-2.

title: JORS: revise metapaper for reviewer round 1
description: Revise the JORS metapaper to address reviewer comments from the April 2026 decision letter. This issue is the parent for the manuscript-content tasks covering motivation, architecture accuracy, figures, quality control framing, reuse, and conclusion updates.

1. lab_archives_mcp-5.2 architecture/tool-surface corrections
2. lab_archives_mcp-5.3 figures and captions
3. lab_archives_mcp-5.1 motivation, related work, implications, conclusion
4. lab_archives_mcp-5.4 QC, reuse, privacy, sample-data, support
5. lab_archives_mcp-5.5 abstract polish if time allows

title: JORS: strengthen motivation, related work, implications, and conclusion
description: Revise the metapaper framing so it reads as a research software paper rather than product documentation. Add a clearer problem statement, explicit objectives or motivating gap, related-work comparison language, broader scientific and institutional implications, and a structured conclusion that summarizes contributions and limitations.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The manuscript states the motivating gap or problem in a way that is recognizably research-oriented rather than purely descriptive.
2. Related-work language positions the software against existing ELN, LIMS, or RAG-style integration approaches without unsupported novelty claims.
3. Broader implications and limitations are discussed in prose suitable for a JORS metapaper.
4. The revised manuscript ends with a structured concluding section that summarizes contributions and constraints.
notes:
Recommended order: 4. Estimate: 150 min. Do after the architecture section and figures are in place so the framing can reference the revised structure accurately.
[orchestrator] Upstream lab_archives_mcp-5 closed in RUN_ID=20260415-155153-db1d7e41 on run/20260415-155153-db1d7e41.

title: JORS: correct architecture section and document complete tool surface
description: Correct the Implementation and Architecture section so it accurately reflects the current MCP server surface. Explicitly document the write layer, including write_notebook_entry and upload_to_labarchives, and make sure the paper no longer understates the available mutating tools or provenance functionality.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The Implementation and Architecture section accurately reflects the current tool surface exposed by the repository.
2. The manuscript explicitly mentions the write/provenance layer, including write_notebook_entry and upload_to_labarchives.
3. Tool categories and component descriptions are internally consistent across the manuscript, README-facing claims, and response letter.
4. Reviewer 3's factual accuracy concern can be answered directly by pointing to revised manuscript text.
notes:
Recommended order: 2. Estimate: 90 min. Start the manuscript revision here because it fixes the clearest factual inaccuracies.

title: JORS: add architecture and workflow figures with captions
description: Add the minimum figure set needed for revision, including at least one architecture figure and, if space permits, a workflow or data-flow figure. Ensure the manuscript actually includes the assets with captions and references in the relevant sections.
acceptance_criteria:
1. At least one architecture figure is included in the manuscript source that will actually be submitted.
2. Figure captions and in-text references explain what the reader should learn from each figure.
3. The figure assets are stored in the repository in a stable location and linked from the manuscript correctly.
4. The response letter can cite the added figure(s) as a direct answer to the reviewer requests.
notes:
Recommended order: 3. Estimate: 120 min. Do after architecture text is stabilized so captions and callouts match the final component story.

title: JORS: expand quality control, reuse, privacy, sample-data, and support text
description: Strengthen the manuscript sections on quality control and reuse. Cover test scope, integration-testing strategy, error handling and validation, any support mechanisms, sample-data references already present in the repository, and the data-residency/privacy implications of external embedding or vector backends such as Pinecone and Qdrant.
acceptance_criteria:
1. The Quality Control and Reuse sections describe test scope, integration strategy, and validation claims with enough specificity for reviewers to assess trustworthiness.
2. The manuscript references the repository's existing sample data or example assets where relevant.
3. Support mechanisms and reuse guidance are stated explicitly rather than left implicit in the repository structure.
4. Privacy and data-residency language distinguishes between local/self-hosted options and third-party embedding or vector services.
notes:
Recommended order: 5. Estimate: 120 min. Depends partly on lab_archives_mcp-2 because QC claims should be tied to collected evidence.

title: Enhance Agent Capabilities with Explicit Semantic Model
description: Implement a robust, graph-based semantic model for the agent to track experiments, findings, and provenance, moving beyond stateless interactions. This references our commitment to an explicitly defined semantic model.

title: Add commit message guidance to AGENTS.md
